description: In an apartment filled with moving boxes, Rachel stands near a doorway while Phoebe stands holding a glass. Rachel says, "It's little. I mean, the the girls brought their dollies to my tea party. I served the best air."
Phoebe lowers her glass slightly.

Phoebe gestures with her free hand and asks, "Why'd you make, like a whole big thing out of, you know, everyone has to hang out in the big apartment?" Monica walks into the room and begins, "Because they took"
Monica continues, "our apartment. I wanted to punish them, but I'm I'm done now. They've suffered enough."
Phoebe looks at Monica.
Phoebe replies, "If you wanted to punish them, you should have just made them hang out here."
Monica nods slightly.
Monica points a finger forward, stating, "True. Alright, then. When I'm finished with this place, it's gonna be ten times better than that place."
Phoebe points toward the hallway and asks, "Oh, are we going to trash their place?"
Chandler walks into the hallway outside the apartment, wearing a black coat, with Joey and Ross standing in the doorway across the hall.
Chandler trips on the raised doorway threshold of the apartment and says, "Steps."
Chandler turns around in the doorway, looking back across the hall toward Joey and Ross.
Chandler points into the opposite apartment and yells, "Slut."
Chandler walks into the room, followed by Ross and Joey. The group gathers near the kitchen table.
Chandler throws his arms wide and announces loudly, "You'll all be very happy to hear that Kathy is sleeping with that guy."
The group looks at Chandler in silence.
Joey steps forward and says, "So you were right." Chandler points a finger at Joey and replies, "I confronted her, and she didn't deny it."
Chandler lowers his hands and turns toward the inner room.
Chandler stops, looks around the living room, and says, "I don't live here,"
Chandler turns and walks out the apartment door, with Joey and Ross following him out into the hallway.
Monica calls out, "Chandler." Chandler steps back into the doorway.
Chandler waits in the doorway.
Monica asks, "Are you sure?" Chandler steps forward and says, "I may be drunk, but I know what she said. So then I went over to Beefsteak," his volume slightly raised.
Ross tilts his head and asks, "Chili's?"
Chandler looks forward, his mouth slightly open.
Chandler points at Ross and says, "Beefsteak Charlie's. Yes."
Chandler raises both hands and says, "See you and I have always been"
Chandler waves both hands rapidly around his head. Ross and Joey cross their arms and watch him.
Chandler stops moving his hands and says, "so sorry."
Chandler pulls a matchbook from his pocket and says, "The bartender taught me a trick, though."
Chandler strikes a match and holds it up between his fingers.
Chandler continues to watch the flame steadily until it burns out.
Chandler drops the burnt match and says, "I don't remember"
Chandler turns and walks out the door, adding, "it's." Joey and Ross watch him leave.
The scene changes to a daytime exterior view of brick apartment buildings. Inside a hallway, Monica, wearing a heavy winter coat, operates a large floor buffer.
Monica struggles as the buffer swings widely across the hallway floor.
Rachel steps out of a nearby apartment door, wearing a black coat and carrying a bag.
The buffer hits the wall, and Monica says, "Whoops."
Rachel asks, "Okay. Do you need help with that?"
Monica turns off the buffer and replies, "No, I got it. I just feel bad. I never vacuum."
Rachel nods, turns, and enters a different apartment door. The setting changes to a kitchen, where Joey and Phoebe are standing near a counter.
Rachel walks in, sets down her bag, and says, "So I was with Joshua for an hour today, and he has not asked me out. Just so frustrating. Why don't you ask"
Joey leans on the counter and continues, "him out?"
Rachel places her hands behind her neck and says, "Oh, yeah, totally. That's such a turn on."
Joey looks at Rachel while Phoebe continues eating.
Joey nods and says, "Doesn't seem desperate. That's the turn on."
Phoebe looks at Rachel.
Phoebe gestures with a spoon and says, "He just got a divorce, right? So he's probably really nervous around women. You know, maybe you just need to make the first"
Phoebe finishes, "move." Rachel gestures with one hand and says, "I've never asked a guy out before." Phoebe's eyes widen and she asks, "You've never asked a guy out?"
Rachel replies, "No. Have you?"
Phoebe answers, "Thousands of times."
Joey raises his eyebrows and looks at Phoebe.
Phoebe raises her spoon and says, "That doesn't make me sound too good, does it?"
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "I don't even know how I would go about it."
Joey steps away from the counter, raising one hand, and says, "Oh, what I do is I look a woman up and down and I say, hey,"
Joey holds his gaze on Phoebe.
Joey dips his head and says in a lower pitch, "how you doing?"
Rachel crosses her arms and looks away.
Rachel says, "Oh, please."
Phoebe turns her attention to Joey.
Phoebe says in a slightly lower pitch, "Hey, how you doing?"
Joey smiles and points a finger at Phoebe, who smiles back at him.
In an apartment kitchen, Rachel stands with her arms crossed.

Rachel uncrosses her arms and steps to the counter. She picks up a cordless phone handset and a piece of paper, saying, "You know what? I'm going to do that. I'm going to call him up and I'm going to ask him out. I can do that. Ask him,"
Rachel holds the phone and presses a button.
Joey and Phoebe stand behind her at a foosball table. Joey asks, "How you doing?"
Rachel brings the phone to her ear. Joey and Phoebe watch her.
Rachel says into the phone, "Hi Joshua? It's Rachel Green from Bloomingdale's."
Rachel listens to the phone.
Rachel brushes her hair behind her ear and says, "Yeah, I was just wondering,"
Rachel looks downward and touches her forehead.
Rachel says, "if you,"
Rachel covers her face with her hand, then lifts her arm, extending her hand slightly.
Rachel points her hand upward and says, "left your wallet at the store today."
Rachel keeps her hand raised.
Rachel points to her temple, then to her ear, saying, "Well, we found a wallet and,"
Rachel lowers her hand.
Rachel adjusts her hair again and says, "the license. Well, that is a good idea."
Rachel looks at the piece of paper in her hand.
Rachel looks at the piece of paper in her hand and says, "Well, let's see. This license,"
Rachel lowers the paper.
Rachel says, "belongs to a Mr."
Rachel turns her head slightly.
Rachel gestures with her free hand and says, "Phoebes."
Phoebe, eating from a plate at the foosball table, smiles.
Rachel says, "Okay, so sorry to bother you at home. I'll see you tomorrow. Bye."
Rachel pulls the phone away from her ear.
Rachel lowers the phone. "You've done that a thousand times," she says quietly to herself.
Phoebe looks up from her plate and says, "I've never done that."
Rachel presses a button on the phone, exhales audibly, and presses the phone to her forehead.
Rachel says, "Oh, God. I just got so nervous that he was going to say no." Joey turns toward her, gesturing with his hands, and says, "Well, you got to give him something he can't say no to. Like,"
Joey continues to gesture.
Joey points at Rachel and says, "Knicks tickets."
Joey gestures with both hands and says, "Invite the guy to a Knicks game, you guaranteed he'll say yes."
Rachel lowers the phone, putting a hand on her hip. "Really? You think that'll work?" she asks. Joey replies, "Absolutely."
Joey gestures and asks, "And if it doesn't, can I get the extra ticket?"
A loud whirring noise begins. Rachel, Joey, and Phoebe turn toward the hallway.
The whirring continues. Rachel walks toward the door and asks, "The heck is that?"
Rachel opens the apartment door and looks out. In the hallway, a man operates a floor buffer, producing the loud whirring sound.
The setting changes to a city skyline across a river with the sun low in the sky.
The setting changes to a different apartment living room. Ross, Joey, and Phoebe sit facing a television, which plays quietly. Chandler walks into the room.
Chandler stops, raises his hands slightly, and asks, "Did she call?"
Joey, Ross, and Phoebe look at Chandler.
Phoebe shakes her head and says, "No." Joey shakes his head slightly and says, "Sorry."
Chandler lowers his hands.
Chandler rubs his head with both hands. "Maybe I should call her," he says. Joey points the TV remote at him and says, "No, forget her, man."
Joey waves his hand, adding, "You don't need her. You don't need that." Ross crosses his arms, nods, and says, "He's right. What she did was."
Chandler lowers his hands from his head.
Chandler rubs his hands together, then gestures outward. "Yeah, but what if I was wrong?" he asks.
Chandler keeps his arms extended.
Ross asks, "How might you be wrong?" Chandler gestures, saying, "Well, you know, what if she didn't actually sleep with the guy?"
Joey sits forward and says, "Dude, tell me she actually told you this." Chandler points at him, replying, "She did,"
Chandler gestures rapidly, saying, "not have to tell me. I saw the play and there was no heat." He points at Ross and says, "Back me up here, Ross."
Ross asks, "That's all you're basing this on?" Chandler gestures again, saying, "That's not backing me,"
Chandler gestures emphatically, saying, "up. Look, you said, you know, with the off stage and the heat and the on stage and".
Joey interrupts, raising his hands, and says, "that was just a theory. There's,"
Joey gestures slightly and says, "a lot of theories that didn't pan out. Lone Gunmen."
Joey continues, "Communism, geometry."
Chandler's jaw drops slightly, and he looks down.
Chandler looks up, says, "Oh, my God," and walks quickly out of the room.
The setting changes to a clothing store office. Rachel stands in the room, holding two tickets.
Rachel practices aloud, "Would you like to go to a basketball game with," gesturing with the tickets.
Rachel looks around the room.
Rachel gestures with the tickets and says, "you know, it's funny, basketball, because I happen to have tickets to,"
Rachel looks at the tickets, holding them up slightly, then looks down, pressing her lips together.
Rachel raises the tickets and says loudly, "Who Likes the Knicks?!"
Rachel remains standing in the office.
In an office dressing room, Joshua stands wearing a black suit. Rachel stands nearby holding a small notepad.
Joshua turns toward Rachel and asks, "What do you think?"
Rachel looks at Joshua, pressing her lips together.
Rachel steps closer, placing a hand on Joshua's shoulder. "Well, as a single woman who is available," she says. Joshua turns fully toward her as audience laughter is heard.
Joshua faces Rachel.
Rachel says, "I think you look great."
Joshua exhales, his shoulders dropping slightly, and looks away.
Rachel says, "Yes," her tone soft.
Rachel continues, "Oh, yeah, you look great."
Joshua looks at Rachel, then turns to face forward again. Rachel keeps her arm extended toward him for a moment before lowering it, looking down and shifting her weight.
Rachel points toward Joshua's suit and says, "It oh, yeah, this looks great."
Joshua turns his head to look at Rachel again.
Joshua asks, "So you like it?" Rachel gestures with her hands and replies, "I do. I love it."

Joshua adjusts his jacket and says, "In fact, I think I'm going to wear it home." Rachel replies, "Great." Joshua turns away to grab his bag, saying, "All right. Well, thank you so much for all your help."
Rachel looks down at her notepad.
Rachel presses her pen to the notepad and says, "Well, I guess this is it."
Joshua picks up his black bag.
Joshua gestures with his hand, saying, "Thanks. Maybe I'll see you in the spring for the bathing suits."
Rachel points toward the door and replies, "Well, you don't want to do that now."
Joshua stops at the doorway, looking back at Rachel.
Joshua says, "That's okay."
Joshua raises his hand in a small wave, saying, "Anyway, hopefully I'll see you around sometime," before turning to walk out.
Rachel raises her hands abruptly and calls out loudly, "Basketball!" Audience laughter is heard.
Joshua steps back into the doorway, turning to look at Rachel.
Joshua asks, "Sorry?"
Rachel walks to a nearby table, picks up two tickets, and turns back toward Joshua.
Rachel holds out the tickets and says, "I have two tickets to the Knicks game tonight, if you're interested."
Rachel extends the tickets further, adding, "Just as a thank you for this week."
Joshua's eyes widen. He points at the tickets, stepping back into the room.
Joshua says, "Wow, that would be great." Rachel asks, "Really?" Joshua takes the tickets from her, replying, "Yeah, that would be fantastic."
Joshua looks at the tickets and says, "My nephew is crazy about the Knicks." Rachel stares at him, her smile fading. Loud audience laughter is heard.
Joshua turns and walks out the door.
Joshua stops in the hallway, holding up the tickets, and says, "This is fantastic."
Joshua points at Rachel with the tickets and says, "Thank you so much." Rachel clasps her hands together in front of her.
An older man in a brown suit enters the doorway behind Joshua. The man says, "Rachel? Good morning."
Rachel looks at the older man and says, "Hi," softly.
The older man nods. The scene changes to an apartment hallway where Chandler approaches a green door labeled "10".
Knocking sounds are heard as Chandler taps the door three times. The door opens inward.
Kathy, wearing a gray shirt, stands in the doorway. Chandler speaks to her and she replies briefly.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "I just wanted to come over to say that I'm sorry." Kathy stands holding the edge of the door.
Chandler places a hand on his chest, saying, "I acted like the biggest idiot in the world. And I can completely understand why you were so upset."
Kathy says, "Oh, wow," looking down and leaning her head against the doorframe.
Kathy looks directly at Chandler.
Kathy says, "I really wish you'd called me." Chandler raises his hands and replies, "Yeah, I know."
Chandler says, "I wish I had, too," while stepping into the apartment.
Chandler continues, "But, you know, I I think this is a good thing, you know, because we've had our first fight and now we can move on." Kathy closes the door behind him.
Chandler stops walking and turns back toward Kathy.
Chandler gestures broadly and says, "I know. For me," as Kathy stands with her hands behind her back.
Kathy looks down, her expression remaining still. Chandler turns and walks further into the living room.
Chandler spots something on a chair, picks up a pair of folded light-colored pants, and holds them up.
Chandler turns back to Kathy and says, "Nick's pants." Audience laughter is heard.
Kathy looks at Chandler.
Kathy says, "Yeah."
Chandler looks down at the pants in his hands, then back at Kathy.
Chandler holds the pants and says, "Well, I think our second fight is going to be a big one." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler stares at Kathy, his jaw slightly tense. Kathy keeps her gaze lowered.
The scene changes to an exterior nighttime view of an apartment building with fire escapes. A yellow taxi drives past a streetlamp below.
Inside an apartment, Joey sits on a coffee table facing Rachel in a recliner. Joey speaks to her. Rachel responds, gesturing with her hands.
Joey gives two thumbs up, then rests his hands together. In the background, Ross leans against a kitchen counter with his arms crossed.
Ross asks, "So the first time you asked a guy out, he he turned you down?"
Rachel quickly turns her head to look at Ross, her mouth slightly open. Joey also looks toward Ross.
Rachel says, "He didn't turn me down."
Ross replies, "He's at the game, isn't he?" Rachel gestures broadly and says, "I got the date. I'm just not on it." Audience laughter is heard.
Ross raises his eyebrows and turns his head away.
The door to the apartment opens, and Monica, wearing a plaid shirt, steps inside.
Monica smiles and says, "Okay, it's ready."
Monica gestures with her head for them to follow.
Monica says, "Come on," turning back toward the hallway. Ross asks, "What's ready?" while walking toward her.
Monica stops in the doorway.
Monica insists, "Just come."

Ross walks out into the hallway. Monica follows him out, leaving the door open. Inside an apartment, Rachel, Ross, Joey, and Phoebe enter a clean and redecorated living room. Monica stands in the center wearing a plaid shirt.
Looking around the room, Rachel says, "Oh, my God."
Phoebe continues to look around the space.
Phoebe says, "Wow."
Audible audience laughter is heard as the group walks further into the living room, their gaze shifting around the walls and furniture.
Rachel walks toward Monica and asks, "Did you work for two days straight?"
Monica replies, "Pretty much."
Monica gestures toward the ground.
Monica asks, "What do you think of the floor?" Joey replies, "I don't know," accompanied by audience laughter.
Joey continues, "It looks the same."
Monica looks at Joey, her expression remaining still.
Monica asks, "You used to have carpet?" Audience laughter is audible.
Joey and Ross answer together, "Yeah."
Rachel and Phoebe walk toward the couch while Monica turns toward the coffee table.
Monica points to a table filled with food and says, "I made snacks."
Monica sits on the couch and lies back. "Please, just hang out, okay? I'm just going to rest my eyes just a little bit," she says.
Ross and Joey walk toward the kitchen area as Monica rests on the couch.
Ross asks, "Mom, do you want us to come back later?" Monica answers with her eyes closed, "Oh, no, stay. Just keep talking."
Monica remains lying on the couch, her head resting on a red pillow.
With her eyes still closed, Monica says, "I am always the hostess," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler opens the front door and walks in, wearing a dark coat over a blue shirt.
Ross asks, "Hey, how'd it go?"
Chandler stands still near the kitchen table.
Chandler says, "Well, she wasn't sleeping with him." Phoebe says, "Oh, good." Audience laughter plays.
Ross places a hand on his chest. Chandler adds, "She is now," prompting more audience laughter.
Ross lowers his hand. Phoebe, Rachel, and Joey stare at Chandler.
Ross asks, "What are you saying?" Chandler gestures with his hand and says, "I'm saying that she is a devil woman."
Chandler points toward Ross.
Chandler gestures with both hands. "You think you know a person. Then they turn around, they sleep with Nick."
Chandler pauses, his facial muscles tight.
Chandler says, "Nick and his rock hard pecs and his giant man nipples," triggering audience laughter.
The group remains silent. Chandler lowers his head and covers his mouth with one hand.
Chandler removes his hand, raising his volume. "I hate him! And I hate her!"
Chandler lowers his volume and says, "I don't hate her. I love her. This is all my fault."
Ross asks, "Really?"
Chandler nods and steps toward a chair.
Chandler sits down. "How is it your fault?" Joey asks.
Chandler replies, "Because I should have known. If I just called her after a big stupid fight, she never"
Chandler continues, "would have gone out with Nick and they wouldn't have ended up in bed together," rubbing his forehead.
Chandler lowers his hand and looks up.
Chandler gestures with one hand and says, "I threw her at his man nipples." Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel steps forward and places a hand on Chandler's shoulder.
Phoebe leans forward. "Honey, this is not your fault. Just because you guys had a fight does not justify her sleeping with someone," she says.
The group is silent. Ross looks downward, then slightly raises his head.
Ross says, "Well, if she thought they were on a break—" Loud audience laughter begins as Ross trails off and the others stare at him.
The setting changes to an office. Rachel sits at a desk. An older man enters the room holding a wallet.
The man walks toward Rachel's desk, looking down at the wallet.
The man says, "Rachel, one of your customers seemed to have left his billfold. A Joshua Bergen," handing her the wallet.
Rachel takes the wallet.
Rachel says, "Really?" The man asks, "Will you call him?" Rachel replies, "Yes, I will. Absolutely," as the man turns and leaves the room.
Rachel opens the wallet and pulls out a small card.
Rachel sets the wallet on the desk and holds up the card in front of her, looking at it closely.
Rachel clears her throat, adjusts her posture, and holds the card with both hands.
Looking at the card, Rachel says softly, "Hello, Rachel."
Rachel turns her head slightly to the side.
Rachel says, "Hi, Joshua."
Rachel holds up the card again.
Rachel lowers her pitch and says, "I left my wallet here on purpose."
Returning to her normal voice, Rachel replies, "Really?"
Rachel slightly lowers the card.
Using the deeper voice, Rachel says, "Yes. I just wanted to see you again." She switches back to her normal voice and says, "Oh, I'm glad," followed by audience laughter.
Rachel smiles while looking at the card.
Using the deeper voice, Rachel says, "Rachel, I'd like to say something to you."
Rachel's smile widens.
Rachel says softly, "Yes."
Rachel lowers her head slightly.
Rachel lowers her pitch and says, "How you doing?" as audience laughter plays.
Rachel rolls her eyes, shakes her head slightly, and then brings the card near her face. Production credits roll over the scene.
The screen turns black.
The screen remains black.
